title: Limit
short: The value a function approaches as its input approaches a point.
full:
The limit of f(x) as x approaches a, written lim_(x→a) f(x), is the single value the outputs close in on as x gets near a — regardless of what happens exactly at a. A function can have a limit at a point where it is undefined; that is precisely the case that makes limits powerful.

Limits underpin all of calculus: the derivative is a limit of average rates of change, and limits at infinity describe the horizontal asymptotes and end behaviour of a graph. When direct substitution gives the indeterminate form 0/0, factoring and cancelling usually reveals the limit.
